posts.body: If you pass dice without rolling them can you ask them back to roll if there are in next players hand?

cole007 wrote:

If you pass dice without rolling them can you ask them back to roll if there are in next players hand?

I thought every player had to roll the dice at the beginning of their turn.  I don't think rolling the dice is optional.
The rules clearly state that you must roll the dice at the beginning of your turn. Hence, the other player should not even accept the dice from you.
A soldier may be played prior to the required dice roll.

What if you forget? I saw the Canada Championship Final that guy forgot to roll after he played Knight and give dices to next player. Son after he wanted it back but that opponent did not want to give him because of the rule that if you gave away dices cant get them back.

cole007 wrote:

What if you forget? I saw the Canada Championship Final that guy forgot to roll after he played Knight and give dices to next player. Son after he wanted it back but that opponent did not want to give him because of the rule that if you gave away dices cant get them back.

I'm not familiar with that particular game, but this is straight out of the rules published here:
https://www.catan.com/understand-catan/game-rules

page 4, turn overview:
"You must roll for resource production."

page 4, the turn in detail:
"You begin your turn by rolling both dice."

Perhaps this is what happened in the Canada game?
"After you're done, pass the dice to the player to your left, who then continues the game with step 1." (Step 1 being "You must roll for resource production.")  Maybe the player wanted the dice back to do something else on their turn, and the 2nd player said no?

Here it is. Watch from 15:30 to 17:00.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L7t2CypaTME
The players violated the rules.  I guess there's no tournament official at the championship to appeal to.  IMHO, that game is invalid.  🙂

Eldard wrote:

The players violated the rules.  I guess there's no tournament official at the championship to appeal to.  IMHO, that game is invalid.  🙂

Need to replay from scratch 😄 😮

There is actually someone in the back in that video who looks like a referee. They seem to agree with the player not returning the dice. Maybe there is already some clarification somewhere but I would have ruled differently. 

The tournament rules state this:
These are clarifications to the standard rules. If there is any disagreement between the standard rules and these clarifications, then the clarification is considered to be the official rule.... A player’s turn begins with receiving the passed dice.

but they also say this:
Games follow CATAN 5th Edition rules. ... If rules are broken at a table, players must call for a referee immediately. The referee’s ruling is final.

Now, you can say that the player's turn started with receiving the dice. However, passing the dice to the player was an illegal move since the resource roll has not happened yet. Since that was appealed in time I would have ruled that the tournament clarification is not superseding the standard rules here. 

Of course, I am not a Catan referee or tournament player. Probably a question that needs an official ruling from Catan Studios. If asked like this:" Would it be possible in a Catan tournament for a player to forego their turn (including resource roll) by simply passing the dice to the next player?", I have a hard time believing it would get a "yes" as response.

Agreed.  Under the rules, the player was not authorized to pass the dice until the player had rolled the dice and resolved the outcome (i.e., players earn appropriate resources; possible robber action).  Playing cards, trading; and building roads/settlements/cities are optional, but the active player must roll in order to determine roll outcomes for all the players.  If I can pass the dice without rolling them, then I don't have to worry about starting my turn with more than 7 cards -- no fear of the Robber.

The game was invalid and should be replayed.

cole007 wrote:

Here it is. Watch from 15:30 to 17:00.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=L7t2CypaTME

Oookay....
So at https://youtu.be/L7t2CypaTME?t=984, player played a knight card, moved the robber, stole a card from an opponent, and then passed the die to the next player.

Commenters here 
https://youtu.be/L7t2CypaTME?t=1003
are saying that since he passed the dice before rolling, "it was ruled that that means you gave up your turn".  Didn't know that was actually a thing! 😮

Had an odd thing like this when playing a tourney of Doiminon, 4p game, somebody noticed in when there were 3 Provinces left that we only had 11 of them in the pile!  The ruling was made to just "play as is" since we were assuming that many were left anyways (except for the player that was card counting, but I digress).

Yes. I agree. On tournament where I was referee same thing with dices happened but  one roll after and because of that the player already rolled his dices and made a move we could not take all back. The play continued. In this video from Canada Championship Final almost immediately after he passed the dices he wanted them back but could not get them because of the rule. 
I can't find that rule.

Agreed.  Under the rules, the player was not authorized to pass the dice until the player had rolled the dice and resolved the outcome (i.e., players earn appropriate resources; possible robber action).  Playing cards, trading; and building roads/settlements/cities are optional, but the active player must roll in order to determine roll outcomes for all the players.  If I can pass the dice without rolling them, then I don't have to worry about starting my turn with more than 7 cards -- no fear of the Robber.

While that should be what happens, in practice, they'll probably just "play as is" since the participates and judges may not have the time to sit through another game.  They may not have those tables available after a set time (may be needed for other matches?)

The play continued. In this video from Canada Championship Final almost immediately after he passed the dices he wanted them back but could not get them because of the rule.

Tournament rules are odd at times.  For one case, if there's a tie, then they just flip a coin to see who wins b/c the game doesn't have a tiebreaker, or it's deemed to be worse than flipping a coin (evidently [shrug])

Been a bit since I played, but doesn't everyone gain from rolling for production? If so, then him not rolling dice for production screws everyone playing. Perhaps he didn't have favorable production numbers?

Subangelis wrote:

Been a bit since I played, but doesn't everyone gain from rolling for production? If so, then him not rolling dice for production screws everyone playing. Perhaps he didn't have favorable production numbers?

It can also hurt players if they're over the hand limit.  Or help but then hurt if you gain an 8th resource, and the next player rolls a 7
